When he called me to gather information about my father's hospitalization, he did not disclose that he would be charging for the call, but that 15 minute call did also appear on my father's bill. He also spoke very slowly on the phone and in the hearing which just made dollar signs spin in my head watching the money go go go... I did express my discontent when my father received the bill. Attorney Murphy did not address all my billing questions, but did agree to lower the bill by 1/10. So that is why he is getting 2 stars instead of 1. I got into my first accident that was not my fault and became distraught about what my first steps should be. Over the course of a week or so, my mother sent me the information for a local law firm that she saw on a commercial, which happened to be Robbin's Law LLC.

I contacted Robbin's Law, LLC and after all information was gathered (which took a minimum amount of time), I was guaranteed that I had a case.

I went into this situation a little apprehensive because of all the horror stories you hear about hit and run's, however, Gavin took all of this anxiety away and inspired me to fight for what I deserved.

Gavin is an upstanding, honest, egalitarian and I couldn't have chosen a better business to get through this trying time with.

I was never left out of the loop, communication was excellent, he is clearly knowledgable as well as passionate about what he does, and you will not regret putting your trust in him.

Gavin will truly make you feel less of a client and more of a friend, but still remaining the professional aspect. I'd like to thank Gavin and his team publicly for helping a novice as myself learn and succeed.

If you are looking for someone to truly bring you justice, who cares and prioritises your needs, and is always professional, this is the ideal place for you.

reviews.text: My fiance and I chose to use Aeton Law Partners (specifically, Wendy Estela) on referral from an attorney family member of ours residing in a different state. We hadn't used lawyers before in our careers, but because we were both making a large career jump to the same practice, we elected to get one to help us with our contracts. Since both of us were joining the same practice (we work in healthcare), one of us being the first specialist of their kind at the location, the hiring process was relatively extensive and involved several months of discussions, emails, and visits before we even saw an offer or contract. You could imagine that we were nervous to select the best individual to help us deliver on the investment. Wendy was not only extremely professional, but also knowledgeable, kind, responsive, and meticulous in advising us. She consistently caught important wording in our document that we had almost dismissed as minutia and brought up good points for general contractual concepts. As it happens, even the smallest changes made huge differences in how we better guaranteed a fair agreement with our new workplace. She spotted things that even the original contract authors found to be astute and worthy of attention/rewording. We appreciated her patience, efficiency, and flexibility in discussing each detail with us in ways that we could understand even though we were not in the field of law. The three of us decided together to avoid involving her in actual contract negotiation in order to encourage a continued positive relationship with our future colleagues, but we felt she prepared us well for things to bring up in the final discussions. We also felt that her price for both sets of contracts was more than fair. We would recommend Wendy and Aeton Law Partners to anyone making a big jump in their careers and wanting to ensure optimal fairness, longevity, and satisfaction with their contracts. Thanks, Wendy!

I had the opportunity to engage Wendy Estela at the law firm of Aeton Law Partners involving a variety of issues including managing investors, dissolution of a company, and generally getting things done efficiently under a tight deadline. reviews.text:
Attorney Meghan Woods took my workman’s comp case, many months after I’d gotten another Attorney from a firm that is constantly advertising on radio and t.v. and of which I figured had to know what they were doing. It became evident to me that the first Firm and their lawyer were totally ineffectual and grossly mishandling my case after 6 months, to the extent they let an important Deposition languish until the respondent filed for discontinuation- because I had ignored a ‘notice of Deposition’ which was part two of a Deposition which began 2 months earlier. The first firm ignored the notice almost costing me the case by the time I received the notice from Worker’s Comp, with days to spare and all because the first Attorney and Firm did not act! My particular case was an incredibly hard case.
My employer,  despite nearly two decades of my  being a diligent, dependable hard worker- rewarding me every single year with written ‘top-notch’ reviews and raises- threw me under the bus after forcing me to do a work order that was physically impossible, and despite my admonition that I ‘did not need to get injured!’ The result? I was injured and am now permanently disabled. Over the next two years and 5 months and thats without counting the initial 6 months with the first  Attorney & Firm, Attorney Meghan Woods and Firm never gave up on me or my case! She and her Firm expertly out maneuvered the opposition representing my former employer, who I might add appeared to be waiting for my demise- thus making them ‘self-absolved’ financially-of my severe and permanent injury until finally a trial was requested by our side, and after many months a date was set. Well, just a little over a week before trial I received a call from Attorney Woods with a ‘Settlement Offer’.. a sizable 6 figure sum which Attorney Woods spent the next week negotiating and renegotiating to obtain a fair figure for me to consider, and which I accepted. I want to note that I spent countless hours on-line studying every aspect of Worker’s Comp per the State of Connecticut and it became apparent that Law Firm’s and Attorney’s who handle Workman’s Comp cases… are virtually some of the hardest working Attorneys in the Industry! And I discovered that my countless hours of reading, studying and saving everything I could find- on my computer to study further- meant very little as sometimes- ‘nothing is what it seems in Worker’s Comp!’ Don’t try to play Attorney. They know what they’re doing. Attorney Meghan Wood’s and the Firm were incredibly responsive, always patient with all my questions, which I occasionally sent via e-mail and unlike the first Firm, treated me like  my case totally mattered and was never ‘put on the back burner!’ Attorney Wood’s has a great personality, is sharp as can be, and most importantly…. While there are no guarantee’s of a case being won or settled, the Attorney/Firm that represents you is the most important aspect in a Worker’s Comp case, especially when they are very serious and complicated cases like mine! Before hiring Attorney Woods and her Firm, I’d met with other Firm’s and Lawyers who told me out right that I had a ‘very tough case’ ( that I believe they didn’t want to take on! ) If there was a 100 Star rating for any reviews, I would give Attorney Woods, Vargas & Chapman & all staff a 100++!

But let me explain to you what happened when my husband died suddenly when we lived in TN.  I left the house for ten minutes and when I left he was sitting at the table. 11 minutes later I walk in and found him on the floor dead.  the grief I was in and still in was beyond any words I can write here. when I went to the funeral home I asked if she knew a lawyer I could get and she asked why I needed a lawyer and I told her well my husband just died and I need to hire a lawyer for our estate. she asked you were married and living together? is everything you own in both of your names and I said yes. she said you do not need a lawyer. everything goes to you, she said I might need to show a death certificate to the bank but the house, cars and anything else goes to me. the weight that was sitting on my shoulders just lifted off. not the grief but it made it so much easier for me moving forward. that is the way it should be in every state. but nope good old CT is making me go through probate (even thought I am listed on the house deed).  so if you need a lawyer I can highly recommend attorney Donovan. The entire office is so helpful and friendly, I cannot praise them enough.  We came away with just what we needed and wanted. We would highly recommend Allaire Elder Law.

There was still a significant amount of work I had to do on my end because legally they couldn't do it for us, but they made the steps very clear. Working with them was worth every penny and I'll never stop recommending them!

I was the family point of contact, and worked with a dedicated attorney and paralegal from the firm throughout the process.  At all times they were easily accessible (e-mail, phone calls, in person), provided practical advice, and got the job done within the projected timeframe.  Having "early" approval is of paramount concern since the family is obligated to pay the skilled nursing home fees prior to approval.  Our family feels that Allaire Law's fee was LOWER than the savings the family enjoyed by having a sound Title 19 application submitted and approved in a reasonable timeframe.  They even negotiated a reduced financial hardship rate for the timeframe prior to Medicaid approval, which softened the blow of that family financial burden.

Dealing with the Federal and State of Connecticut agencies is complex and confusing at times.  Attorney Allaire’s very highly skilled staff members were wonderful in guiding us through each phase of the journey.  Charlotte proved to be an able and effective team leader and coordinator. Her guidance and counsel proved so valuable to us all. Daniella was our lifeline to the office.  When questions arose, she answered our calls and emails quickly.  Her home care visits and assessments reassured our Mom that things would work out well.  Because of her caring nature, she interacts with seniors in a kind, gentle and gracious way.  Linda was wonderful in obtaining Veterans Administration benefits and then terminating them when Medicaid was granted. She worked tirelessly to achieve her goals, which were unfortunately made more complicated by the agency itself.  Irene was responsible for the application and the granting of Medicaid. Her wonderful sense of  humor combined with her years of experience in that field, worked wonders. Whenever I was confused or frustrated with the volumes of paperwork, she assured me things would work out well, and she was always right.  Our Mom is still living at home, happily, and we have to thank the wonderful staff members of Allaire Elder Law for their skill, knowledge and expertise in making this happen.
